releases.markdown:
# CMS v1.27.0

_2026-07-22 · changes since r-1.26.0_

## Features
- Chat agents get a full-page editor at Admin → Chat Agents, covering the system
  prompt, model, tool allowlists, limits, and guest-chat appearance settings.
- Guest conversations are now stored per agent and browsable (with JSON export)
  under the agent's new Conversations tab — review exactly what visitors asked
  and how the agent answered, tool calls included.

## Fixes
- When an external API rejects a request, chat agents and the admin
  test-data-source tool now see the upstream status and the API's own error
  message instead of a generic "request could not be completed" — so an agent
  can correct a bad parameter instead of retrying blindly.
- Optional API query parameters left empty are now omitted from the request
  instead of being sent as empty values, which some APIs reject.

# CMS v1.26.0

_2026-07-21 · changes since r-1.25.4_

## Features
- **Guest chat agents.** A new *Chat Agents* admin page lets you configure chatbots that talk to logged-out visitors on your published pages. Each agent has its own system prompt, model, welcome message, usage limits, and an explicit allowlist of the data-source requests and collections it may touch.
- **GuestChat block.** Place a chat agent on any page from the Page Builder — as an inline panel or a floating bubble. The visitor never sees or chooses the agent's model, prompt, or tools: the server re-reads the block from the published page and loads its configuration.
- **Safe by default for guests.** A guest bot's queries only ever see published content, and anything it creates or updates lands as a draft for you to review. Per-IP and per-site rate limits (per minute, per day, per conversation) cap abuse and cost, and token usage is counted per day.
- **The admin assistant can manage agents.** Ask the AI assistant on the Chat Agents page to list, create, update, or delete an agent; it has a built-in playbook covering limits, allowlist shapes, and the safety model.
- **More file types in the media gallery.** PDF, plain text, Markdown, CSV, and JSON uploads are now accepted alongside images. Files whose type the browser doesn't report — `.md` most often — are recognised from their filename instead of being rejected.

# CMS v1.25.4

_2026-07-21 · changes since r-1.25.3_

## Fixes
- "Sign in with BizbeeCMS" works again on deployed Sites. The login page built
  the SSO return URL with `http://` (the in-Worker proxy hop mis-reports the
  protocol), which the manager rejected with `badReturn`. The return URL is now
  always `https://`.

## Other
- OpenRouter is now the sole AI provider. The Workers AI fallback and the AI
  Gateway plumbing were removed — every Site deploys with an OpenRouter key, so
  the fallback was unreachable. If a Site somehow has no key, the AI assistant
  endpoints now respond 503 instead of silently switching providers.

# CMS v1.24.0

_2026-07-07 · changes since r-1.23.0_

## Features
- Per-Site `robots.txt`: a settings editor with a safe default (crawlers allowed, `/admin` `/api` `/preview` disallowed) plus a free-text override and an auto `Sitemap:` pointer.
- IndexNow: publishing, unpublishing, deleting or renaming a page automatically notifies search engines of the changed URLs (per-Site key served at a fixed path).
- 301 redirects: auto-captured when a page is renamed, plus a manual redirects admin UI.
- Per-page SEO controls: a `noindex` toggle (also excludes the page from the sitemap and IndexNow), full Open Graph / Twitter cards, per-Site search-engine verification tokens (Google / Bing / Yandex), and automatic BreadcrumbList JSON-LD for nested pages.
- JSON-LD authoring: a JSON-LD component kind with a Develop editor (kind toggle, template editor, live JSON preview), List → schema.org `ItemList` aggregation, and an on-demand JSON-LD authoring guide for the AI.
- `/llms.txt` AI-crawler index and per-page `.md` variants for AI crawlers, with an editable `llms.txt` template (variable slots) and settings editor.
- SEO audit report: an admin page flagging orphan pages, broken internal links, missing meta, and missing image alt text — now scanning inside referenced component trees too. Plus AI tools to audit and bulk-fix missing meta and missing alt text.
- Responsive images: width variants served from `/media?w=`, `srcset`/`sizes` on rendered images, image dimensions captured at upload and stamped through for CLS stability (including AI-generated images).
- Open Graph images: auto-generated OG images with fallback serving and a regenerate button.
- A designated branded 404 page that renders in the visitor's URL locale, and a per-Site rate-limit threshold (Off / Normal / Strict) enforced at the edge on public page paths.

## Fixes
- Content-locales save now purges the sitemap and `/llms.txt` caches.

# CMS v1.23.0

_2026-07-07 · changes since r-1.22.0_

## Features
- URL-based content locales: published pages now take their language from the URL prefix (e.g. `/fi/...`) instead of a cookie. The Language Switcher navigates to the prefixed URL, and `<html lang>` matches the URL's locale for correct SEO.
- Per-locale page slugs: each page can have its own slug per language, so localized URLs read naturally in each language. Internal links and the Language Switcher reverse-resolve to the right localized path, and sibling-slug collisions are guarded on save and in AI `create_page`.
- SEO: public `sitemap.xml`, plus canonical and `hreflang` alternate tags that account for localized slugs.
- Edge caching: opt a page in to Cloudflare edge caching with a per-page max-age. Publish / unpublish / delete and content-changing edits purge the cache automatically (per-page and shared-tag).
- Operator guide documenting URL locales, localized slugs, SEO, and the edge-cache opt-in / purge behavior.

## Fixes
- Published pages no longer carry the admin UI's locale/message data into their cached payloads (kills an Accept-Language cache-poisoning path and a large payload bloat).
- `/sitemap.xml` is no longer wrongly stamped with a page's Cache-Control from a top-level wildcard page; root dotted files (sitemap/robots/llms.txt) are excluded from the edge-cache gate.

# CMS v0.11.0

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.10.0_

## Features
- Collections: import and export items as CSV or JSON, from a new operator UI.
- Collections: a schema manager to add, drop, and rename fields, with atomic live-store rebuilds.
- Collections: AI tools to drop and rename collection fields (schema evolution).
- Collections: an operator raw-SELECT SQL console (fenced SELECT-only, not exposed to the AI).
- AI chat panel resizes via a top-left pointer-drag handle (replacing the native CSS resize).
- Chat: scroll-to-latest pill with follow-bottom-only transcript behavior.
- Chat: tool cards get a show-more toggle for truncated input/output blobs.
- Chat: inline active-override label near the input (PM-SSO).
- Accessibility: focus trap and initial focus in the open chat dialog, Esc minimizes the panel, and focus-visible rings on widget buttons.

## Fixes
- Collections: fix collection-create failing when the CREATE TABLE spanned multiple lines (use prepared statement instead of D1 exec).
- Collections: restore the missing Collections nav-item icon.
- Chat: fix the model-picker crash on stale-cache rows from /api/chat/models.
- Chat: fix the expand/shrink toggle getting stuck one-way (toggle on size, not preset).

# CMS v0.10.0

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.9.2_

## Features
- AI chat assistant panel is now resizable, with default/half-screen preset toggles.
- Chat input is a resizable textarea with a switch for Enter behavior (send vs. newline).
- The model picker shows per-model prices (input/output USD per 1M tokens) and input-modality icons, and is filtered to tool-call-capable models only.
- Your selected model now persists across reloads (validated against the live catalog).
- The chat widget shows the remaining credit on the in-use OpenRouter key.
- Tool calls are persisted in chat history and rendered as cards (name shown once, with an input/output accordion).
- The launcher shows an unread badge when minimized and a new reply arrives.
- PM-SSO operators can export a chat as the full model-payload JSON.
- PM-SSO operators can edit system-prompt version overrides from the debug panel, backed by a new prompt-version store and gated prompts API.

## Fixes
- Accept 48-byte `CMS_AUTH_SECRET` values: the AES key is now SHA-256-derived instead of being rejected by the secret-box KEK.

# CMS v0.9.2

_2026-06-24 · changes since cms-v0.9.1_

## Fixes
- **"Sign in with BizBeeCMS" SSO button now always shows on the admin login.** It was hidden unless you arrived from ProjectManager via a specific link, so opening `/admin` directly (or after an apex→www redirect) showed only email/password. The button now appears whenever the Site is wired to ProjectManager.

# CMS v0.8.0

_2026-06-23 · changes since cms-v0.7.0_

## Features

### Per-site OpenRouter API key (new)
- Each site can now use its **own** OpenRouter API key for the AI assistant, set in ProjectManager → Site settings (write-only field: shows "key set" / "no key", never reveals the stored value, with a "Clear key" action).
- The key is **encrypted at rest** in ProjectManager (AES-256-GCM), decrypted only at deploy time, sent to the deployer over HTTPS, and installed as a real **secret** on that site's CMS Worker — never a plaintext variable, never written to a deploy log.
- Graceful fallback at every step: a site with no key (or a key that can't be decrypted) falls back to the deployer's global OpenRouter key, and if that's also unset, to the built-in Workers-AI provider. A missing key never breaks a deploy.

## Other
- New ProjectManager secret `SITE_SECRET_KEY` (the encryption key) and a `sites.openrouterApiKeyEncrypted` column + migration.
- The deployer now sets the CMS Worker's OpenRouter key via `wrangler secret put` instead of a build-time variable.

# CMS v0.7.0

_2026-06-22 · changes since cms-v0.6.0_

## Features

### Content collections (new)
- Define your own data collections — each becomes a real database table, created safely behind a hardened SQL fence (no raw SQL ever reaches the table layer).
- Full admin UI: create and edit a collection's schema with a field-type picker, then manage its items in a table with the right input per type (date/time, number, select, boolean, multiselect, rich text).
- Structured search, filter, sort and pagination over collection items.
- Bind collection data into pages: a single block can pull one record's fields into its props, or a new built-in **List** block can stamp a component once per matching record — all authored visually in the page builder, with graceful empty states.
- AI assistant can create collections, add/update/archive items, query data, and wire up component bindings — all through structured tools, never raw SQL.

### CMS-local accounts & roles (new)
- Sign in directly in the CMS with email + password, **Sign in with Google**, or PM single-sign-on — no more forced redirect to ProjectManager.
- Four-role hierarchy (SuperAdmin / Admin / Manager / Editor) with server-enforced permissions on every admin route.
- Invite teammates by email (sent via Cloudflare Email) with a role-limited invite form; accept-invite flow creates the account and signs them in.
- User-management screen: list users and pending invites, change roles, and remove users — all gated by who's allowed to do what.

### Manage a site from your own AI tools (new)
- Each deployed CMS exposes a remote MCP server at `/mcp`, so you can connect Claude Code (or any MCP client) and manage the site with your own model.
- Mint and revoke per-site API keys in **Settings → API Keys**, with a copy-paste "Connect Claude Code" snippet.

### Component kits
- Tag components, then export everything with a tag as a single installable kit bundle — and import a kit in one step.

### AI provider
- The AI assistant now runs on OpenRouter by default (with the built-in Workers-AI provider as fallback), and its model picker lists the full OpenRouter catalog.

## Other
- Hardened the runtime SQL fence with a parsing validator and an attack-string test corpus; added a schema-rebuild planner for future field drop/rename.
- Numerous database migrations for the new auth, roles, tags, API-key, invite and collection tables (applied automatically on deploy).
- Trilingual (EN/FI/ET) coverage for every new screen.
